verifier_notes: Fresh verifier rerun. composer test passes 30/87 post-wave-4 (kiloclaw parallel landed). RED artifact shows 5 errors + 1 failure on the six webhook tests with 5 validateToken tests green. Red.txt mtime precedes production service mtime. validateToken body byte-identical to pinned snapshot (pre-build diff empty, live re-diff empty). All 8 prior tasks PASS. No em dashes, no mocks, no network calls, autoload clean.
commands_run: awk extract registerWebhook body + grep forbidden substrings
awk extract validateToken body + diff vs pre-build snapshot
grep U+2014 on three authored files

criteria.reason: phpunit-red.txt has EXIT=2, shows 30/30 executed with 5 Errors + 1 Failure. All six locked webhook test names appear as failing (Errors 1-5 by method name + Failure on test_register_webhook_client_exception_returns_failed). RED captured before service body landed: red.txt mtime 1776160316 < service.php mtime 1776160328.

Red run reports 30 tests executed, 65 assertions, 5 Errors + 1 Failure. Only the 6 new webhook methods failed; 24 dots preceding the failure block show the 5 validateToken tests passing alongside OnChainOS, KiloClaw, AgentDeployer, and smoke tests. 11+ tests executed requirement satisfied (30).

TelegramHttpClient.php defines both getMe and setWebhook. Test file's FakeTelegramHttpClient implements setWebhook returning configurable response. Red run loads without fatal (30 tests executed, 24 passed), proving the dual change landed before the RED run.
phpunit-green.txt shows 'OK (30 tests, 87 assertions)' with EXIT=0. All six new webhook method names are in the test file and no failures reported.
Live composer test rerun returns exit 0 and 'OK (30 tests, 87 assertions)'. 30 >= 20 tests and 87 >= 20 assertions.

TelegramHttpClient.php has <?php, declare(strict_types=1), namespace App\Services\Telegram, interface TelegramHttpClient, and exactly two methods: getMe(string $token): array and setWebhook(string $token, string $webhookUrl): array. No extra methods.
TelegramTransportException.php unchanged: strict_types, namespace App\Services\Telegram, final class TelegramTransportException extends \RuntimeException, empty body.

Service has <?php, declare(strict_types=1), namespace App\Services, use of TelegramHttpClient + TelegramTransportException, final class TelegramBotRegistrarService, constructor-promoted readonly TelegramHttpClient $client, validateToken(string $token): bool, registerWebhook(string $token, string $webhookUrl): array. 'public function ' count is exactly 3 (constructor, validateToken, registerWebhook).
validate-token-diff.txt is 0 bytes. Live re-diff of awk-extracted validateToken body against pre-build-validate-token-body.txt is empty (DIFF_EMPTY_OK). Body matches pinned spec snapshot verbatim.

test_register_webhook_empty_token_returns_invalid_no_client_call calls registerWebhook('', 'https://example.com/hook'), asserts status=invalid, webhook_url=null, error='empty token', and asserts $fake->setWebhookCallCount === 0. Service returns this exact shape for empty trimmed token.
Same test also calls registerWebhook('   ', 'https://example.com/hook') and asserts the same invalid/empty-token shape plus setWebhookCallCount === 0. Both '' and '   ' literals present.

test_register_webhook_non_https_url_returns_invalid_no_client_call uses http://example.com/hook, asserts status=invalid, webhook_url=null, error contains 'https', setWebhookCallCount === 0. Service rejects non-https prefix without calling client.

test_register_webhook_invalid_host_returns_invalid_no_client_call uses https://nohostdot, asserts invalid, webhook_url=null, error contains 'host', setWebhookCallCount === 0. Service strips 'https://' and checks for '.'; 'nohostdot' has no dot so returns 'webhook url host invalid'.

test_register_webhook_happy_path_returns_registered seeds setWebhookNextResponse=['ok'=>true,'result'=>true], assertSame exact array ['status'=>'registered','webhook_url'=>'https://example.com/hook','error'=>null], plus setWebhookCallCount===1 and setWebhookLastArgs===['123:abc','https://example.com/hook'].

test_register_webhook_client_exception_returns_failed seeds setWebhookNextException = new TelegramTransportException('transport boom'), wraps call in try/catch with fail('leaked'), assertSame ['status'=>'failed','webhook_url'=>'https://example.com/hook','error'=>'transport boom']. Service catches TelegramTransportException $e and returns $e->getMessage().

test_register_webhook_telegram_rejected_returns_failed seeds setWebhookNextResponse=['ok'=>false,'error_code'=>400,'description'=>'Bad Request: bad webhook'], asserts exact ['status'=>'failed','webhook_url'=>'https://example.com/hook','error'=>'Bad Request: bad webhook']. Service branches on ok!==true and returns description.

awk-extracted registerWebhook body: 0 matches for ' new ', '::', ' static '. Exactly 1 occurrence of 'https://' literal (the str_starts_with prefix check). 0 occurrences of 'http://' in service file. Catch clause uses TelegramTransportException class name without ::.
grep for createMock|getMockBuilder|MockObject on test file returns no matches.

Test file defines 'final class FakeTelegramHttpClient implements TelegramHttpClient' with both 'public function getMe(string $token): array' and 'public function setWebhook(string $token, string $webhookUrl): array'.

FakeTelegramHttpClient declares all eight recorder fields: $getMeCallCount, $getMeLastToken, $getMeNextResponse, $getMeNextException, $setWebhookCallCount, $setWebhookLastArgs, $setWebhookNextResponse, $setWebhookNextException.
php -l on TelegramHttpClient.php, TelegramBotRegistrarService.php, and TelegramBotRegistrarServiceTest.php all report 'No syntax errors detected' with exit 0.

Autoload probe: all 8 FQCNs resolve (ok: AgentDeployerService, KiloClawClientService, OnChainOSPaymentService, TelegramBotRegistrarService, Telegram\TelegramHttpClient, Telegram\TelegramTransportException, OnChainOS\OnChainOSClient, OnChainOS\OnChainOSException).

grep file_get_contents|curl_init|curl_exec|fsockopen|fopen('http on three authored files returns no matches. Only allowed https literals present: 'https://' prefix literal in service + test fixture URLs.
grep for U+2014 (em dash) on all three authored files exits 1 (no matches).

All 8 prior tasks report verdict_overall_status: PASS via task_loop.py status: bootstrap-proof-loop, scaffold-service-stubs, test-harness, landing-mockup-steve-ive, tdd-telegram-validate-token, wizard-mockup-steve-ive, tdd-onchainos-create-charge, agents-list-mockup-steve-ive.

The three authored files for this task are the spec-permitted targets: app/Services/Telegram/TelegramHttpClient.php (extended), app/Services/TelegramBotRegistrarService.php (extended, validateToken byte-identical), tests/Unit/Services/TelegramBotRegistrarServiceTest.php (extended with promoted fake + 6 new tests). Parallel kiloclaw task changes under app/Services/KiloClaw/ and related are out of scope for this AC per verifier instructions.
